title: Understand: Voting, Rebels, Debt, and Congressional Power
description: Check your understanding of Section 5 of the Fourteenth Amendment with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: The Fourteenth Amendment: Citizenship, Freedom, and Equality
Voting, Rebels, Debt, and Congressional Power
Understand: Voting, Rebels, Debt, and Congressional Power

assessment_items.question_content:
**What issue does Section 2 of the Fourteenth Amendment address?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The apportionment of representatives in the U.S. House
The citizenship of anyone born in the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
**Congressional Republicans worried about which of the following after the Civil War and the abolition of slavery?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The southern states would receive more representation in Congress, as Black Americans were now counted as whole persons.
The southern states would receive fewer seats in Congress, as they were forced to give up some political power in order to return to the Union.
The southern states would receive more seats in the Senate, as Black Americans were now counted as whole persons for purposes of Senate representation.
The southern states would see no change in political power after the Civil War, as the overall population did not change.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Under Section 2 of the Fourteenth Amendment, if a state denies Black men who are 21 or older the right to vote, that state’s representation in Congress will \_\_\_\_.**


[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which group of people was affected by Section 3 of the Fourteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following best explains the purpose of Section 3 of the Fourteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To prevent people who rebelled against the United States from holding office
To enshrine birthright citizenship for all people, regardless of race
To guarantee voting rights protections for formerly enslaved people
To give Congress the power to enforce the other clauses of the Fourteenth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
**Section 4 of the Fourteenth Amendment dealt with which issue?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which TWO statements best describe what Section 4 of the Fourteenth Amendment states?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The U.S. government rejects the war debt of the Confederate States of America.
There can be no question about the validity of the debt of the U.S. government.
Any person who has rebelled against the U.S. government cannot serve in office.
All persons born in the United States are guaranteed citizenship and all of the rights associated.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the purpose of Section 5 of the Fourteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress is granted the power to pass laws to enforce the other provisions of the Fourteenth Amendment.
Congress could raise the debt ceiling without question, as all national debt is valid under the law.
State governments must not violate key rights enumerated in the Bill of Rights.
The federal government’s laws are superior to the laws passed by state governments.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Section 5 of the Fourteenth Amendment is also known as the  \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**According to the Supreme Court, what are TWO limits to Congress’s enforcement powers under Section 5 of the Fourteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Congress cannot raise the debt ceiling.

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to Section 3 of the Fourteenth Amendment, who is prohibited from serving in federal office?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Those who participated in an insurrection.
Those who were not born in the United States.
Those who do not practice Christianity.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Section 2 of the Fourteenth Amendment addressed Republican concerns about the impact of the abolition of slavery on which of the following clauses in the U.S. Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
